title: 3 Insurance Stocks for High Total Returns
author: Bob Ciura
description: The article highlights three insurance stocks with strong dividend growth potential: Aflac, Principal Financial Group, and The Hanover Insurance Group. These companies demonstrate solid financial performance, consistent earnings growth, and attractive dividend histories.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong Q3 2025 results with 60.7% revenue surge, 43 consecutive years of dividend increases, expected 2025 earnings of $7.45 per share, and consistent earnings-per-share growth of 8.5-9.9% over past years
Increased assets under management to $784 billion, 13% year-over-year earnings-per-share growth, solid 6-7% average annual earnings-per-share growth, and successful asset management business
Significant Q3 2025 net income improvement from $102.1 million to $178.7 million, strong pricing momentum, profitable growth across business segments, and disciplined cost management

title: Principal Financial Stock Lags Industry: Should You Hold or Fold?
author: Zacks
description: Principal Financial Group's stock has underperformed its industry and the broader market over the past year. While the company faces rising expenses, it is expected to benefit from strategic initiatives, strong retention, and favorable capital deployment. The article suggests that current investors should hold the stock.

title: FBLA Seeks Volunteers to Inspire the Next Generation of Business Leaders at Career Connections Conferences
author: Fbla
description: FBLA is seeking volunteers to support its upcoming Career Connections Conferences in Atlanta and Des Moines, which will allow participants and their companies to build their talent pipeline and prepare the next generation of business leaders.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company is participating in the FBLA Career Connections Conferences, indicating their interest in supporting and engaging with the next generation of business leaders.

title: Reasons Why Investors Should Retain Principal Financial (PFG)
author: Zacks Equity Research
description: Principal Financial (PFG) remains poised to gain from favorable claims and disciplined expense management, positive net cash flow, growth in business and a solid capital position.
